overview.description: Are our not_null / unique tests doing anything, and where are we missing some? This tool answers that for a dbt project — statically, without touching the warehouse — by propagating each test's guarantee through the column lineage.

systems.title: ① Guarantee Model
systems.description: The shared vocabulary — what a guarantee, a verdict, and a finding's evidence ARE. Tiny but load-bearing: every other system speaks this language.

systems.title: ② Inputs
systems.description: Where the facts come from: the column lineage (via a cache over the upstream engine) and the declared dbt tests parsed from the manifest.

systems.title: ③ Propagation Core
systems.description: The brain: push each declared guarantee through the lineage's transforms to a per-column verdict, soundly. The hardest, highest-value code in the repo.

systems.title: ④ Reporting
systems.description: Turn verdicts + declared tests into actionable findings (REDUNDANT / MISSING / UNCOVERED / CONTRADICTION) plus coverage, leverage, consolidation, and cost.

systems.title: ⑤ CLI
systems.description: The two commands a person runs: `report` (read it) and `check` (CI gate). Wires Inputs → Propagation Core → Reporting together.
